data.body: [G3 child-coverage, this child as link-consumer half of parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6] Re-verified against whole roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-consumer). 4-bucket: SC1/AC4 (shared gate consumed by BOTH paths) jointly FULLY covered — 908c covers the link outbound path (`_diff_links` managed-ref-gated REMOVE), ba2c covers the parent CLEAR path; AC2 (survives compaction) behaviorally covered by 908c AC5 'survives a compaction boundary' + ba2c 'across a compaction boundary'. AC1/AC3/AC5/AC6 (reducer fold, byte-parity, reclamation, docs) are STRUCTURAL — parent Scope explicitly retains these as its OWN in-scope primitive-build deliverables ("the reducer field + compaction preservation + byte-parity; the shared gate helper; reclamation; docs"), delegating only the two CONSUMERS to children. No uncovered parent criterion attributable to children. Clean for G3.
